recent.title: Mother Root <> FERMAT | Weekly Check In
recent.summary: Maddie confirmed that the funnels are finalized and will be deployed on Meta on Monday, with results expected by the end of next week. She also plans to build a Mother's Day funnel for a specific product, targeting the UK market, featuring a bundled offer with a slight discount. Talia offered to provide examples of promotion-based bundles.
action_items: Send examples of promotion-based bundle funnels for inspiration
Review the Mother's Day bundle or offer if sent ahead of time
Check on Monday morning when funnels go live

recent.title: Mother Root <> FERMAT | Weekly Check In
recent.summary: Maddie and Talia confirmed Mother Root's plan for a FERMAT vs. Replo test, with Maddie showcasing her progress on the funnel build. Talia provided detailed optimization feedback, including adding CTAs, removing the 'view on site' link, and improving upsell presentation. They discussed technical issues like font display and sticky add-to-cart behavior.
action_items: Double-check font appearing in shopping bag section
Investigate background color on static add-to-cart button
Flag sticky add-to-cart bar disappearing issue to team
Conduct final review of funnel build and send recommendations
Confirm process for swapping funnel on live Meta link

recent.title: Mother Root <> FERMAT | Weekly Check In
recent.summary: Maddie from Mother Root sought assistance with technical aspects of the Funnel Builder, including adjusting product page badges and 'per serve' descriptors, and raised significant concerns about the cart's UX/UI and the platform's ability to optimize funnels for both desktop and mobile simultaneously. The lack of desktop optimization is a known product roadmap item and a potential blocker for their evaluation.
action_items: Flag cart UI issues (sizing of title and price) to product team
Share examples of aesthetically pleasing and top-performing funnels
Investigate using HTML to optimize desktop/mobile within a single experience
Keep buyer informed about product roadmap for native desktop optimization

calls.analysis: MOTHER ROOT has had regular weekly check-ins with Talia Cohen (now transitioning to Enterprise team). The brand was actively building and testing FERMAT funnels against Replo, with Maddie Carey leading the effort. Key concerns revolved around desktop optimization and design customization limitations. The account has now signaled intent to churn (March 26 Slack message).

messages.text: Hey Pulkit Talia Cohen - I hope you are both well. I'm writing to inform you that we will not be moving forward with using Fermat on a long term basis - happy to provide feedback if it is of interest. Please could you let me know best way to go about cancellation and the associated date the platform will therefore be shut down for us? I appreciate all of your help up until this point.

Hey Mother Root team! I'm Pulkit, your new GM here at FERMAT. I've been with the FERMAT team for about 1.5 years and I'm genuinely so excited to start working with you all! Would love to hop on a call and get to know everyone. Feel free to grab some time on my calendar.

Hi Maddie - welcome back to the office. I'm reaching out with some bittersweet but exciting news - later this week I'll be transitioning into a new role on our Enterprise team here at FERMAT. I'm thrilled to introduce Pulkit, one of our outstanding Onboarding and Growth Managers! Pulkit will be following up shortly with a calendar link.
Hi Talia Cohen Do you know if we have any Marmalade landing page live? We've had a couple random orders appear that we can't seem to find out where from.
Hey Tom - yes, all good to go here! Looks Maddie Carey set the traffic split on the FERMAT link to send 0% to FERMAT with 100% to the site. Would you like me to adjust that to a 50/50 split?
We've got paid ads live using the Fermat link
Talia Cohen Hey Talia, are we all good to go live for the Mother's Day funnel?
Hey Talia - I'm OOO for the next two weeks but the channel will be monitored if any issues
Hey Talia - This product isn't appearing in Fermat - I've resynced too. Could you take a look please? MOTHERSDAYSET
Hi Maddie - by any chance, can you meet a half hour earlier today? I have a team call thrown on my books.
Hi Maddie - hope you're having a great week! Checking in on status of your first test. Also sharing Module Visibility Controls update - you can now show/hide specific modules based on device.
Hi Maddie - wanted to follow up on our conversation from last week: how are the final pieces of the funnel coming together?
Hi Maddie, I've made additional changes to the Desktop version of the funnel. Sharing two launch strategy options: 1) Device-based split test, 2) Mobile-first launch. Recommend Option 2.
Additionally, I've created the funnel TC Desktop | Replo Duplication. Our team is still working on the label issue in the variant selector.

Hey Maddie - thanks again for the time yesterday! Sharing a brief recap. Fixed per serve pricing, located variant badges, identified cart/product card design issues. Desktop optimization still needed.
Hey Talia - RHUBARB Q1 LAUNCH DATES: [10th March] Retention Early Access via Email, [26th March] General Launch via Ads
Hi Maddie - thanks for your time earlier today! Some follow up: confirmed contract addendum shifting opt-out date to 4/22. Sharing 90 Day Roadmap. Aim to have first two funnels ready in 10 days.

slack.analysis: CRITICAL: MOTHER ROOT has formally notified of intent to churn on March 26, 2026. The Slack channel shows a deteriorating engagement pattern - Maddie was OOO for 2 weeks (Feb 20 - Mar 10), during which the GM transition from Talia to Pulkit occurred. After returning, no call was booked despite Pulkit's outreach. The traffic split was set to 0% FERMAT / 100% site, suggesting the brand was already disengaging from the platform. The cancellation message came just 16 days after the GM transition.

messages.snippet: Mother Root <> FERMAT | Weekly Check In Event. Calendar invite for weekly meeting transferred to Pulkit.

email.analysis: Very limited email activity for MOTHER ROOT in the last 30 days. Only a Linear notification and a calendar invite transfer from Talia to Pulkit. The lack of direct email correspondence with the brand contacts (Maddie, Tom) is concerning and aligns with the disengagement pattern visible in Slack.
